content: Define the synthesizable SystemVerilog module TopModule with the exact required port names, directions, and widths. Do not add, remove, or rename any ports.
source:
Implement a module named TopModule with the following interface:

- input  logic        clk
- input  logic        reset
- input  logic        in_valid
- input  logic [15:0] in_a
- input  logic [15:0] in_b
- input  logic [1:0]  in_mode
- output logic        out_valid
- output logic [31:0] out_y
- output logic [7:0]  out_tag

[Strict Interface Contract]
Your generated TopModule must keep the exact port names and widths below.
Do not rename ports. Do not add/remove ports. Do not change bit-widths.

content: Implement one internal 8-bit sequential issue-tag counter signal. On reset, clear it to 0. On each positive clock edge, increment it by 1 only when in_valid is asserted, so it tracks the next tag to issue for accepted inputs.
source: out_tag is an issue tag that starts from 0 after reset and increments by 1 for every accepted input (in_valid=1).

content: Implement the combinational 32-bit add result signal for mode 2'b00 using zero-extension of in_a and in_b before addition. Keep this as a parallel candidate result for performance-oriented out_y selection.
source:
Computation function (32-bit out_y):
   - mode 2'b00: zero-extended add:      {16'd0, in_a} + {16'd0, in_b}

Optimization direction: performance-first.
- reduce critical combinational depth

content: Implement the combinational 32-bit multiply result signal for mode 2'b01 using zero-extension of in_a and in_b before multiplication. Keep this as a parallel candidate result for performance-oriented out_y selection.
source:
Computation function (32-bit out_y):
   - mode 2'b01: zero-extended multiply: {16'd0, in_a} * {16'd0, in_b}

Optimization direction: performance-first.
- reduce critical combinational depth

content: Implement the combinational 32-bit xor result signal for mode 2'b10 using zero-extension of in_a and in_b before xor. Keep this as a parallel candidate result for performance-oriented out_y selection.
source:
Computation function (32-bit out_y):
   - mode 2'b10: zero-extended xor:      {16'd0, in_a} ^ {16'd0, in_b}

Optimization direction: performance-first.
- reduce critical combinational depth

content: Implement the combinational 32-bit weighted-sum result signal for mode 2'b11 as the sum of zero-extended in_a shifted left by 1 and zero-extended in_b shifted left by 2. Keep this as a parallel candidate result for performance-oriented out_y selection.
source:
Computation function (32-bit out_y):
   - mode 2'b11: weighted sum:           ({16'd0, in_a} << 1) + ({16'd0, in_b} << 2)

Optimization direction: performance-first.
- reduce critical combinational depth

content: Implement one combinational 32-bit selected-result signal for out_y input. Select among the four precomputed candidate result signals based only on in_mode, using a complete selection that covers all four modes.

Implement the sequential out_valid register. On reset, drive out_valid to 0. Otherwise, on each positive clock edge, assign out_valid directly from in_valid so an accepted input at cycle N produces out_valid at cycle N+1, and an invalid input at cycle N produces out_valid low at cycle N+1.
source:
Use a 1-cycle pipeline from accepted input to output.
When in_valid is 1 at cycle N, out_valid must be 1 at cycle N+1 and out_y/out_tag must match that input.
When in_valid is 0 at cycle N, out_valid must be 0 at cycle N+1.

Optimization direction: performance-first.
- keep valid/tag timing clean
- preserve exact 1-cycle pipeline behavior

content: Implement the sequential out_tag register. On reset, clear out_tag to 0. Otherwise, on each positive clock edge, when in_valid is asserted, load out_tag with the current issue-tag counter value so the tag corresponding to the accepted input appears with out_valid one cycle later. When in_valid is deasserted, hold out_tag unchanged because out_valid will be low on the following cycle.
source:
Use a 1-cycle pipeline from accepted input to output.
When in_valid is 1 at cycle N, out_valid must be 1 at cycle N+1 and out_y/out_tag must match that input.
out_tag is an issue tag that starts from 0 after reset and increments by 1 for every accepted input (in_valid=1).

Optimization direction: performance-first.
- keep valid/tag timing clean
- preserve exact 1-cycle pipeline behavior

content: Implement the sequential out_y register. On reset, clear out_y to 0. Otherwise, on each positive clock edge, when in_valid is asserted, load out_y with the selected 32-bit computation result for the current input, so the result appears exactly one cycle after the accepted input. When in_valid is deasserted, hold out_y unchanged because out_valid will be low on the following cycle.
